questions.text: Which one of the following statements is not correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Offspring produced by asexual reproduction are called clones
options.explanation: Correct. Asexual reproduction involves the production of genetically identical offspring, known as clones.

options.text: Microscopic and motile asexual reproductive structures are called zoospores
options.explanation: Correct. Zoospores are small, motile structures produced by some organisms as part of their asexual reproductive process.

options.text: In potatoes, bananas, and ginger, the plantlets arise from the internodes present in the modified stem
options.explanation: Not correct. In these plants, reproduction typically occurs through other means, such as tubers in potatoes, rhizomes in ginger, and corms in bananas.

options.text: Water hyacinth, growing in standing water, drains oxygen from water leading to the death of fishes
options.explanation: Correct. Water hyacinth can contribute to oxygen depletion in water bodies, negatively affecting aquatic ecosystems and leading to the death of fish.

questions.explanation: Potato, banana, and ginger propagate vegetatively by their modified stems. Potato propagates by tuber which has buds over its eyes or nodes. These buds produce new plantlets. Banana and ginger propagate with the help of rhizomes which also have buds on nodes for the formation of new plantlets. Cloning is the process of producing individual organisms with identical or virtually identical DNA, either by natural or artificial means. In nature, some organisms produce clones through asexual reproduction. In simple context, clone can are a single cell or a group of cells that are genetically identical to its ancestor cell or group of cells.Zoospores use a flagellum for locomotion and are created by some protists, bacteria, and fungi to propagate themselves.Water hyacinth is a type of invasive floating plant found in water bodies across the world. These invasive species block the sunlight reaching and oxygen level in water systems, which results in damaging water quality and seriously affecting various lifeforms in the ecosystem.

questions.text: Which one of the following generates new genetic combinations leading to variation?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Vegetative propagation is a type of asexual reproduction that produces progeny by any vegetative propagule (rhizome, tubers, suckers, etc.) without gamete formation and fertilization of male and female gametes.
Parthenogenesis is a natural form of asexual reproduction in which the growth and development of embryos occur without fertilization by sperm and only an egg(female gamete) is required.

Sexual reproduction involves the formation and fusion of male and female gametes. Gamete formation is accomplished through meiotic cell division which involves crossing over between non­sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes leading to new genetic recombination in gametes. Random fusion of these male and female gametes leads to genetic variability in the offspring which although resemble their parents also exhibit new traits of their own.

The occurrence of more than one embryo in a seed is referred to as polyembryony. The Nucellar polyembryony is the process in which an apomictic procedure is involved where many tenderly consequent embryos stand up from the nucleus and surround the emerging zygotic embryo. Thus, we can say that nuclear polyembryony is observed in the members of the citrus family.

questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction involves the formation and fusion of male and female gametes. Gamete formation is accomplished through meiotic cell division which involves crossing over between non­sister chromatids of homologous chromosomes leading to new genetic recombination in gametes. Random fusion of these male and female gametes leads to genetic variability in the offspring which although resemble their parents also exhibit new traits of their own. Vegetative propagation is a type of asexual reproduction that produces progeny by any vegetative propagule (rhizome, tubers, suckers, etc.) without gamete formation and fertilization of male and female gametes. Parthenogenesis is a natural form of asexual reproduction in which the growth and development of embryos occur without fertilization by sperm and only an egg(female gamete) is required. The occurrence of more than one embryo in a seed is referred to as polyembryony.

questions.text: In ginger, vegetative propagation occurs through:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A bulbil is a small, young plant that is reproduced vegetatively from axillary buds on the parent plant's stem or in place of a flower on an inflorescence. These young plants are clones of the parent plant that produced them—they have identical genetic material.
A runner is a slender stem that grows horizontally along the ground, giving rise to roots and aerial (vertical) branches at specialized points called nodes.

The rhizome is a thickened, underground, dorsoventral stem that grows horizontally at a particular depth within the soil. It is brown and shows cymose branching. It can be distinguished from the modified root by the presence of nodes, internodes, terminal buds, axillary buds, and scale leaves. The rhizomes are perennial and propagate vegetatively. They store food materials and appear tuberous. E.g., Zingiber officinale (ginger), Curcuma longa (turmeric), Canna indica.
An offset (also called a pup) is a small, virtually complete daughter plant that has been naturally and asexually produced on the mother plant.

questions.explanation: The rhizome is a thickened, underground, dorsoventral stem that grows horizontally at a particular depth within the soil. It is brown in color and shows cymose branching. It can be distinguished from the modified root by the presence of nodes, internodes, terminal buds, axillary buds, and scale leaves. The rhizomes are perennial and propagate vegetatively. They store food materials and appear tuberous. E.g., Zingiber officinale (ginger), Curcuma longa (turmeric), Canna indica. A bulbil is a small, young plant that is reproduced vegetatively from axillary buds on the parent plant's stem or in place of a flower on an inflorescence. These young plants are clones of the parent plant that produced them—they have identical genetic material. A runner is a slender stem that grows horizontally along the ground, giving rise to roots and aerial (vertical) branches at specialized points called nodes. An offset (also called a pup) is a small, virtually complete daughter plant that has been naturally and asexually produced on the mother plant.

questions.text: In oogamy, fertilization involves:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: A small non­-motile female gamete and a large motile male gamete
options.explanation: The female gamete is large, not small and the male gamete is small, not large.

options.text: A large non-­motile female gamete and a small motile male gamete
options.explanation: Oogamy is the sexual reproduction involving the formation and subsequent fusion of a large, usually stationary, non-motile, female gamete and a small motile male gamete. The female gamete may contain nourishment for the development of the embryo, which is often retained and protected by the parent organism.

options.text: A large non-­motile female gamete and a small non­motile male gamete
options.explanation: The female gamete is non-motile but the male gamete is motile.

options.text: A large motile female gamete and a small non­-motile male gemete
options.explanation: The female gamete is non-motile and the male gamete is motile.

questions.explanation: Oogamy is the sexual reproduction involving the formation and subsequent fusion of a large, usually stationary, non-motile, female gamete and a small motile male gamete. The female gamete may contain nourishment for the development of the embryo, which is often retained and protected by the parent organism. A. Female gamete is large not small and male gamete is small, not large. C. Female gamete is non motile but male gamete is motile. D. Female gamete is non motile and male gamete is motile.

questions.text: During regeneration, modification of an organ to another organ is known as:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Morphogenesis refers to the shaping of an organism by embryological processes of differentiation of cells, tissues, and organs and the development of organ systems according to the genetic “blueprint” of the potential organism and environmental conditions.

Epimorphosis replaces a lost organ of the body by proliferating new cells from the surface of the wound or injured part. Regeneration of an appendage in an arthropod, arm in a starfish, and tail in a lizard occurs by the process of epimorphosis.

Morphallaxis involves the reconstruction of the whole body from a small fragment by reorganizing the existing cells. The regenerated organism is smaller than the original one, e.g., Amoeba. However, after the completion of the process, it grows and attains normal size after some time.

Accretionary growth refers to the growth in the post-embryonic stage or adult stage, where the cells are unable to divide further as a result of differentiation. But at certain locations, some cells can divide mitotically and replace the worn-out differentiated cells when needed.

questions.explanation: There are two mechanisms of regeneration: morphallaxis and epimorphosis. (i) Morphallaxis involves the reconstruction of the whole body from a small fragment by reorganizing the existing cells. The regenerated organism is smaller than the original one, e.g., Amoeba. However, after the completion of the process, it grows and attains normal size after some time. (ii) Epimorphosis replaces a lost organ of the body by proliferating new cells from the surface of the wound or injured part. Regeneration of an appendage in an arthropod, arm in a starfish, and tail in a lizard occurs by the process of epimorphosis. Morphogenesis refers to the shaping of an organism by embryological processes of differentiation of cells, tissues, and organs and the development of organ systems according to the genetic “blueprint” of the potential organism and environmental conditions. Accretionary growth refers to the growth in the post-embryonic stage or adult stage, where the cells are unable to divide further as a result of differentiation. But at certain locations, there are some cells that are able to divide mitotically and replace the worn-out differentiated cells when needed.

questions.text: The process of a series of changes from larva to adult after embryonic development is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incorrect. Regeneration refers to the ability of an organism to replace or repair damaged or lost body parts.
Incorrect. Growth generally refers to the increase in size or number of cells in an organism. It is not specific to the transformation from larva to adult.
Correct. Metamorphosis is the process of transformation or development from an immature larval stage to an adult stage with distinct morphological and physiological changes.
Incorrect. Aging refers to the process of growing old or the progressive decline in physiological function over time. It is not specifically related to the transformation from larva to adult.

questions.explanation: Metamorphosis is a process of a series of changes of form from larva to adult after embryonic development. It is the process by which an animal physically develops after birth or hatching, involving a conspicuous and relatively abrupt change in the animal's body structure through cell growth and differentiation. Regeneration is defined as the replacement, repair, or restoration of the lost or damaged structures or reconstitution of the whole body from a small fragment of it during the post­embryonic life of an organism. Growth is the result of greater anabolic (synthetic) processes over the catabolic (destructive) processes in the organism. It is a permanent irreversible increase in size, weight, shape, and structure usually accompanied by a permanent change of form. Aging may be defined as the progressive deterioration in the structure and functions of the cells, tissues, and organs of an organism with the advancing age.

questions.text: Flowers which have a single ovule in the ovary and are packed into inflorescence are usually pollinated by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Correct. Flowers with a single ovule and arranged in an inflorescence are often adapted for insect pollination, and bees are common pollinators attracted to such flowers.

Incorrect. Wind-pollinated flowers typically have features like lightweight pollen and are not commonly packed into inflorescences. Wind-pollinated flowers are usually less showy and lack attractive features.

Incorrect. Bats are often associated with night-blooming flowers and are generally attracted to larger, fragrant flowers. However, the characteristic of having a single ovule in the ovary is not specifically indicative of bat pollination.

Incorrect. Water is not a common pollinator for flowering plants. Water-pollinated plants usually have small, inconspicuous flowers that release their pollen directly into the water for transport. The description given suggests adaptations for animal pollination rather than water pollination.

questions.explanation: A single ovule in the ovary and flowers packed into inflorescence are characteristics of wind-pollinated flowers. Pollen are light weight and non-sticky which leads to easy dispersal of the pollen from anther to stigma of the flower by wind.

questions.text: A dioecious flowering plant prevents both:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Autogamy refers to the fusion of two gametes that come from one individual.Geitonogamy is the fertilization of a flower by pollen from another flower on the same (or a genetically identical) plant.

Xenogamy is the transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of a different plant. This is the only type of cross-pollination that during pollination brings genetically different types of pollen grains to the stigma. Geitonogamy is the fertilization of a flower by pollen from another flower on the same (or a genetically identical) plant.

Cleistogamy means the formation of flowers that do not open, and thus the production of seeds is a result of autogamy. Xenogamy is the transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of a different plant. This is the only type of cross-pollination that during pollination brings genetically different types of pollen grains to the stigma.

Autogamy refers to the fusion of two gametes that come from one individual. Xenogamy is the transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of a different plant. This is the only type of cross-pollination that during pollination brings genetically different types of pollen grains to the stigma.

questions.explanation: Dioecious plants are those plants in which male flowers and female flowers are borne on different plants. Therefore, they prevent both autogamy and geitonogamy. Autogamy refers to the fusion of two gametes that come from one individual. Geitonogamy is the fertilization of a flower by pollen from another flower on the same (or a genetically identical) plant. Xenogamy is the transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of a different plant. This is the only type of cross-pollination that during pollination brings genetically different types of pollen grains to the stigma. Cleistogamy means the formation of flowers that do not open, and thus the production of seeds is a result of autogamy.

questions.text: Double fertilization is exhibited by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incorrect. Algae typically do not exhibit double fertilization. In algae, fertilization involves the fusion of gametes to form a zygote, but the specific process of double fertilization is a characteristic feature of certain higher plants.

Incorrect. Fungi do not exhibit double fertilization. Fungi reproduce through the formation of spores or by sexual reproduction involving the fusion of specialized reproductive structures, but the process is different from double fertilization.

Correct. Double fertilization is a unique feature of angiosperms (flowering plants). It involves the fusion of one male gamete with the egg cell to form the zygote and another male gamete with two polar nuclei to form the endosperm.

Incorrect. Gymnosperms, which include conifers and cycads, do not exhibit double fertilization. They have a different fertilization process where two male gametes are involved in the formation of the zygote, but there is no formation of endosperm through double fertilization.

questions.explanation: Double fertilization is the characteristic feature of angiosperms. Here, two male gametes are released in the embryo sac or female gametophyte. One male gamete fuses with the egg cell to form a zygote that gives rise to the embryo whereas the other male gamete fuses with the two polar nuclei to form a triploid 3n nucleus in the center of large central cell of the embryo sac. This large cell will give rise to endosperm, a food storing tissue. STB page #99 FTB page # 182- 184

questions.text: Pollination in water hyacinth and water lily is brought about by the agency of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Water does not help in pollination.

In aquatic plants with emergent flowers e.g., water lily and water hyacinth, pollination takes place by wind or insects.Emergent plants are rooted with stiff or firm stems and stand above the water surface.
Insects help in pollination but birds do not.
Pollination occurs through wind or insects.

questions.explanation: In aquatic plants with emergent flowers e.g., water lily and water hyacinth, pollination takes place by wind or insects. Emergent plants are rooted with stiff or firm stems and stand above the water surface.

questions.text: The ovule of an angiosperm is equivalent to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The ovule of an angiosperm is equivalent to a megasporangium which consists of 2 synergids, 1 egg, 3 antipodal cells, and a secondary nucleus.
Megasporophyll is a type of leaf-like structure produced by heterosporous plants such as seed plants, lycophytes, and some ferns.
Megaspore mother cell (2n) gives rise to the ovule.
Spores produced by megasporangium are called megaspores.

questions.explanation: The ovule of an angiosperm is equivalent to a megasporangium which consists of 2 synergids, 1 egg, 3 antipodal cells, and a secondary nucleus. B. Megasporophyll is a type of leaf-like structure produced by heterosporous plants such as seed plants, lycophytes, and some ferns. C. Megaspore mother cell (2n) gives rise to the ovule. D. Spores produced by megasporangium are called megaspores.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is not true?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Pollen grains of many species cause severe allergies
options.explanation: True. Pollen from certain plants can trigger allergic reactions in susceptible individuals.

options.text: Stored pollen in liquid nitrogen can be used in crop breeding programs
options.explanation: True. Storing pollen in liquid nitrogen can preserve genetic material and is used in crop breeding programs to maintain genetic diversity and facilitate controlled pollination.

options.text: Tapetum helps in the dehiscence of anther
options.explanation: Not true. The tapetum is a tissue in the anther that nourishes developing pollen grains, but it does not play a direct role in the dehiscence (opening) of the anther. Dehiscence is often influenced by other factors like moisture and changes in environmental conditions.

options.text: Exine of pollen grains is made up of sporopollenin
options.explanation: True. The exine, the outer layer of pollen grains, is composed of a durable substance called sporopollenin, protecting the pollen during its journey from the anther to the stigma.

questions.explanation: Tapetum is the innermost wall layer of microsporangium that nourishes developing pollen grains. A. This is correct as pollen grains of many species cause allergies like asthma. B. This is correct as pollen stored in liquid nitrogen can be used in crop breeding. D. Spore(pollen grain) of fern plant has two layers exine and intine. Exine is made up of sporopollenin.

questions.text: Seed formation without fertilization in flowering plants involves the process of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Somatic hybridization involves the in vitro fusion of protoplasts to form a hybrid cell and culturing the hybrid cell to form a hybrid plant. There are three aspects of somatic hybridization: fusion of protoplasts, selection of hybrid cells, and identification of hybrid plants.

Apomixis is an asexual reproductive process in plants that does not involve gametic fusion. In apomictic flowering plants, there is no fertilization and embryos develop simply by division of a cell of ovule.
Sporulation is the process of dispersal of spores either by wind or animals.
Budding is a type of asexual reproduction in which a bud develops on some part of the body and starts growing, eventually detaching itself from the parent body and starting an independent life.

questions.explanation: Apomixis is an asexual reproductive process in plants that does not involve gametic fusion. In apomictic flowering plants, there is no fertilization and embryos develop simply by division of a cell of ovule. A. Somatic hybridization involves the in vitro fusion of protoplasts to form a hybrid cell and culturing the hybrid cell to form a hybrid plant. C. Sporulation is the process of dispersal of spores either by wind or animals. D. Budding is a type of asexual reproduction in which a bud develops on some part of the body and starts growing, eventually detaching itself from the parent body and starting an independent life.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is not correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Pollen germination and pollen tube growth are regulated by chemical components of pollen interacting with those of the pistil
options.explanation: Correct. The process of pollen germination and pollen tube growth is indeed regulated by chemical interactions between the pollen and the pistil.

options.text: Some reptiles have also been reported as pollinators in some plant species
options.explanation: Correct. Some plants are adapted to being pollinated by reptiles, such as certain lizards or turtles, in addition to the more common insect or bird pollination.

options.text: Pollen grains of many species can germinate on the stigma of a flower, but only one pollen tube of the same species grows into the style
options.explanation: Not correct. Typically, only pollen of the same species will germinate on the stigma, and multiple pollen tubes may compete to reach the ovule, ensuring fertilization.

options.text: Insects that consume pollen or nectar without bringing about pollination are called pollen/ nectar robbers.
options.explanation: Correct. Pollen/nectar robbers are insects that steal pollen or nectar from flowers without providing a pollination service in return.

questions.explanation: Pollen­pistil interaction is the group of events that occur from the time of pollen deposition over the stigma to the time of pollen tube entry into the ovule. It is a safety measure to ensure that illegitimate crossing does not occur. Pollen grains of a number of plants may settle over a stigma. The pollens belonging to the same species would germinate while others fail to do so but the pollen tube of the compatible pollen will grow through the style to reach the ovule whereas growth of incompatible pollens will be arrested at stigmatic disc or sometimes in the beginning part of the style. A. This is correct as chemical components of pollen regulate pollen germination and pollen tube growth. B. Animals(including reptiles) help in the dispersal of pollen grains. D. This is correct as insects that consume pollen or nectar without bringing about pollination are called pollen/ nectar robbers.

questions.text: Filiform apparatus is a characteristic feature of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Incorrect. The aleurone cells are typically involved in the storage and secretion of proteins in seeds.

Correct. The filiform apparatus is a structure found in the synergids, which are cells in the female gametophyte (embryo sac) of flowering plants. It helps guide the pollen tube to the egg cell during fertilization.
Incorrect. The generative cell is part of the male gametophyte (pollen grain) and is involved in the formation of sperm cells.
Incorrect. The nucellar embryo refers to an embryo that develops from the nucellus tissue in the ovule and is not associated with the filiform apparatus.

questions.explanation: The filiform apparatus is a mass of finger-like projections of the wall into the cytoplasm. It is present in synergids (help cells) of the embryo sac, in the micropylar region. It guards the pollen tube inside the ovule towards the embryo sac. A special cellular thickening present in the synergids at the micropylar end is called filiform apparatus. After fertilization when the egg cell gives rise to a zygote, they get disorganized. The major function is to guide the entry of pollen tubes into synergids and to release sperm cells.

questions.text: In angiosperms, microsporogenesis and mega­sporogenesis:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Both microsporogenesis (in the male reproductive organs) and megasporogenesis (in the female reproductive organs) in angiosperms involve meiosis. Meiosis is a type of cell division that reduces the chromosome number by half, leading to the formation of haploid cells.

Megasporogenesis occurs in the ovule, which is part of the female reproductive structure of the flower. It is the process that gives rise to megaspores, which eventually develop into female gametophytes.
Microsporogenesis occurs in the anther, which is the male reproductive organ of the flower. It is the process that leads to the formation of microspores, which develop into male gametophytes.

Both microsporogenesis and megasporogenesis involve further divisions after meiosis. In microsporogenesis, microspores undergo mitotic divisions to form pollen grains, which contain male gametophytes. In megasporogenesis, one of the four megaspores produced by meiosis typically survives and undergoes mitotic divisions to form the female gametophyte within the ovule.

questions.explanation: In angiosperms, microsporogenesis i.e., formation of microspores (or pollen grains) occurs by the meiotic divisions of diploid microspore mother cells (or pollen mother cells ). Microsporogenesis takes place in the anther. Megasporogenesis i.e. formation of megaspores occurs by the meiotic divisions of diploid megaspore mother cells. Megasporogenesis takes place in the ovule.

questions.text: Flowers are unisexual in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: China rose is bisexual which means males and females are present on the same plant.
Onion is bisexual. Male and female present on same plant.
Pea is bisexual. Male and female present on the same plant.
In cucumber, unisexual flowers i.e., separate male and female flowers are present on the same plant (monoecious plant)

questions.explanation: In cucumber, unisexual flowers i.e., separate male and female flowers are present on the same plant (monoecious plant) A. China rose is bisexual which means male and female present on the same plant. B. Onion is bisexual. Males and females are present on the same plant. C. Pea is bisexual. Males and females are present on the same plant.

questions.text: Coconut water from a tender coconut is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Innermost layers of the seed coat
options.explanation: The innermost layers of the seed coat do not contain coconut water. The seed coat primarily protects the seed, and coconut water is found within the coconut's inner cavity.
Coconut water does not originate from the degenerated nucellus. The nucellus is part of the ovule, but it doesn't contribute to the formation of coconut water.
Coconut water is not derived from the immature embryo. The embryo is present within the coconut but does not produce the liquid known as coconut water.

Coconut water is primarily the liquid endosperm. In coconuts, the endosperm is a tissue that provides nourishment to the developing embryo. In the case of coconut water, it is the liquid found in the endosperm before it solidifies into coconut meat.

questions.explanation: Coconut has multicellular endosperm (called coconut meal) in the outer part and free nuclear as well as vacuolate endosperm (called coconut milk or coconut water) in the centre. A. Inner part of coconut is fruit of the coconut which is composed of two layers , outer(exocarp) and inner (endocarp). B. This is incorrect as free nuclear endosperm is the coconut water. C. Immature embryo cannot be coconut water.

questions.text: Which one of the following fruits is parthenocarpic?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Jackfruit is not typically parthenocarpic. It usually requires pollination and fertilization for fruit development.

Banana is parthenocarpic, meaning it can develop fruit without fertilization. In the absence of pollination and seed formation, the fruit still develops, making it seedless. This characteristic is advantageous for commercial banana production as it ensures uniform fruit quality.
Brinjal is not parthenocarpic. It usually requires pollination and fertilization for fruit development.
Apples are not parthenocarpic. They generally require fertilization and seed formation for fruit development.

questions.explanation: Parthenocarpic fruits are fruits that are formed without fertilization. These fruits are naturally seedless, e.g., banana

questions.text: Male gametophyte in angiosperms produces:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Single sperm and two vegetative cells
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.
This option is incorrect.

options.text: Two sperms and a vegetative cell
options.explanation: The protoplast of the male gametophyte divides mitotically to produce two unequal cells — a small generative cell and a large vegetative cell. The generative cell divides later into two non­motile male gametes (or sperms ). Thus, the male gametophyte in angiosperms produces two sperms and a vegetative cell. The vegetative cell, later on, grows to produce a pollen tube.

options.text: Single sperm and a vegetative cell.
options.explanation: This option is incorrect.

questions.explanation: The protoplast of the male gametophyte divides mitotically to produce two unequal cells — a small generative cell and a large vegetative cell. The generative cell divides later into two non­motile male gametes (or sperms ). Thus, the male gametophyte in angiosperms produces two sperms and a vegetative cell. The vegetative cell, later on, grows to produce a pollen tube. FTB page# 182-184 STB page# 172-174

questions.text: Which one of the following may require pollinators, but is genetically similar to autogamy?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Apogamy refers to the development of a sporophyte from a gametophyte without the occurrence of fertilization. It is a form of asexual reproduction and is not related to pollination.

Cleistogamy is a reproductive strategy where a flower remains closed and self-pollination occurs within the closed flower. While this process is similar to autogamy (self-pollination), it may still involve mechanisms to ensure cross-pollination, such as by utilizing the wind or small insects. Cleistogamous flowers are typically small and inconspicuous.
Geitonogamy involves the transfer of pollen from one flower to another on the same plant. It is a form of cross-pollination but occurs within the same individual.
Xenogamy is cross-pollination between different individuals of the same species. It involves the transfer of pollen from the flower of one plant to the flower of another plant.

questions.explanation: Geitonogamy involves the transfer of pollen from one flower of a plant to the stigma of another flower of the same plant. As the pollen has to move from one flower to another flower, it requires a pollinating agent. Yet it is genetically similar to autogamy, as both the flowers of the plant, share the same genotype of the plant. A. A type of reproduction occurs in some ferns, in which sporophyte develops from gametophyte without fusion of gametes. B. Self-fertilization that occurs within a permanently closed flower. D. Fertilization between genetically different flowers on different plants.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is true?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: The flowers pollinated by flies and bats secrete foul odor to attract them
options.explanation: This statement is true. Flowers that are pollinated by flies and bats often produce foul or putrid odors to attract these specific pollinators.

options.text: Honey is made by bees by digesting pollen collected from flowers
options.explanation: This statement is not entirely accurate. Bees collect nectar from flowers, not pollen, and transform it into honey through a process that involves regurgitation, evaporation, and enzymatic activity.

options.text: Pollen grains are not rich in nutrients
options.explanation: This statement is not true. Pollen grains are, in fact, rich in nutrients. They contain proteins, lipids, vitamins, and minerals, serving as essential food for the development of the pollen tube and the fertilization process.

options.text: Pollen grains of some plants cause severe allergies and bronchial afflictions in some people
options.explanation: Pollen allergies, known as hay fever or allergic rhinitis, can be triggered by the inhalation of airborne pollen grains. Some individuals may experience allergic reactions such as sneezing, runny nose, and bronchial issues in response to certain types of pollen.

questions.explanation: A. Flowers pollinated by insects are large, colorful, and secrete fruity odor. B. Honeybees transform saccharides (carbohydrates) into honey by regurgitating it a number of times, until it is partially digested. The bees do the regurgitation and digestion as a group. After the last regurgitation, the aqueous solution is still high in water, the process continues by evaporation of much of the water and enzymatic transformation. C. Pollen grains are rich in nutrients. D. This is true as pollen grains cause allergies.

questions.text: The hilum is a scar on the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The style is a part of the female reproductive organ of a flower, and its presence is not associated with the hilum on the fruit.
The micropyle is a small opening in the seed coat that allows for water absorption during germination. It is distinct from the hilum.
The hilum is a scar on the seed coat where the funicle (the stalk that attaches the ovule or seed to the placenta within the ovary) was attached during seed development.

options.text: Fruit, where it was attached to the pedicel
options.explanation: The attachment of the fruit to the pedicel is not related to the hilum. The hilum specifically refers to the scar on the seed.

questions.explanation: Ovule is an integumented megasporangium found in spermatophytes which develops into seed after fertilization. An angiospermic ovule is typically an ovoid and whitish structure. It occurs inside the ovary where it is attached to a parenchymatous cushion called the placenta either singly or in a cluster. The ovule is stalked. The stalk is called funiculus or funicle. The point of attachment of the body of the ovule with the funiculus is known as the hilum. It is present as a scar on a mature seed.

questions.text: Transmission tissue is a characteristic feature of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A dry stigma does not have specialized transmission tissue; it is not associated with the transport of pollen tubes.
Similar to a dry stigma, a wet stigma also lacks specific transmission tissue and is not adapted for the transport of pollen tubes.
The style is the part of the female reproductive organ (pistil) that connects the stigma to the ovary. A hollow style does not necessarily have specialized transmission tissue.

The correct option. A solid style typically possesses specialized transmission tissue that facilitates the transport of pollen tubes from the stigma to the ovary, aiding in the process of fertilization in plants.

questions.explanation: Style is traversed by the pollen tube to reach the ovule.It is of two types ­ hollow and solid. In hollow styles, the stylar canal is lined by glandular cells, which are usually multinucleate and polyploid whereas solid style has a core of transmitting tissue, composed of thin walled cells, through which, the pollen tube moves.

questions.text: Which one of the following shows isogamy with non­flagellated gametes?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Sargassum is a brown alga that reproduces sexually. However, it does not exhibit isogamy with non-flagellated gametes. Instead, it displays oogamy, where large non-motile eggs are fertilized by small motile sperm.

Ectocarpus is a brown alga that also reproduces sexually. It does not demonstrate isogamy with non-flagellated gametes either. Ectocarpus exhibits oogamy, where two morphologically distinct types of gametes are produced: larger non-motile eggs and smaller motile sperm

Ulothrix is a filamentous green alga that undergoes both sexual and asexual reproduction. In sexual reproduction, Ulothrix exhibits isogamy, where gametes of similar size and structure (isogametes) are produced. However, the gametes in Ulothrix are flagellated, so it does not match the condition of non-flagellated gametes.

Spirogyra is a filamentous green alga that reproduces both sexually and asexually. In sexual reproduction, Spirogyra demonstrates isogamy, where gametes of similar size and structure (isogametes) are produced. Importantly, the gametes of Spirogyra are non-flagellated, making it the correct option for the given question. The isogametes of Spirogyra come into contact, fuse, and form a zygote.

questions.explanation: A. Sargassum: Sargassum is a brown alga that reproduces sexually. However, it does not exhibit isogamy with non-flagellated gametes. Instead, it displays oogamy, where large non-motile eggs are fertilized by small motile sperm. B.Ectocarpus: Ectocarpus is a brown alga that also reproduces sexually. It does not demonstrate isogamy with non-flagellated gametes either. Ectocarpus exhibits oogamy, where two morphologically distinct types of gametes are produced: larger non-motile eggs and smaller motile sperm C. Ulothrix: Ulothrix is a filamentous green alga that undergoes both sexual and asexual reproduction. In sexual reproduction, Ulothrix exhibits isogamy, where gametes of similar size and structure (isogametes) are produced. However, the gametes in Ulothrix are flagellated, so it does not match the condition of non-flagellated gametes. D.Sexual reproduction in algae takes place through the fusion of two gametes. In Spirogyra, the gametes are similar in size (isogamy) and non­ flagellated (non­motile).

questions.text: Geitonogamy involves:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Fertilization of a flower by the pollen from another flower of the same plant
options.explanation: Geitonogamy is the transfer of pollen grains from one flower's anther to the stigma of another flower on the same plant by a pollinator. It is a self-pollination process. For example, maize.

options.text: Fertilization of a flower by the pollen from the same flower
options.explanation: Autogamy is the fertilization of a flower by pollen from the same flower. It is also a self-pollination process. For example, wheat, rice, etc. Generally happens in closed flowers like cleistogamous flowers that do not require a pollination agent to pollinate.

options.text: Fertilization of a flower by the pollen from a flower of another plant in the same population
options.explanation: Xenogamy is the transfer of pollen from one flower to another flower of a different plant. It is a cross-pollination process. For example, broccoli, squash, etc.

options.text: Fertilization of a flower by the pollen from a flower of another plant belonging to a distant population
options.explanation: Fertilization of a flower by the pollen from a flower of another plant in the same population is also a cross-pollination process.For example, tomatoes, pumpkins, and apples.

questions.explanation: Geitonogamy is the pollination taking place between the two flowers of the same plant or genetically similar plant. Hence, genetically it is self-pollination but since the agency is involved, it is ecologically cross-pollination.

questions.text: An aggregate fruit develops from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This would lead to a different type of fruit, a syncarpous fruit, where the carpels are fused into a single structure. An aggregate fruit specifically arises from an apocarpous gynoecium.

An aggregate fruit forms from a single flower with a multicarpellary (having multiple carpels) apocarpous (free, separate carpels) gynoecium. Each carpel develops into a separate small fruit, and all these individual fruits collectively form the aggregate fruit.
An aggregate fruit is not formed from a complete inflorescence. An inflorescence is a group or cluster of flowers, and the type of gynoecium structure determines the fruit type.
While a multicarpellary superior ovary is associated with some types of fruits, it does not specifically define an aggregate fruit. The critical factor is the apocarpous nature of the gynoecium.

questions.explanation: An aggregate fruit or etaerio is a group of simple fruitlets that develop from free ovaries (apocarpus condition) of a single flower (single gynoecium).

questions.text: Pollen tablets are available in the market for:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In vitro fertilization typically refers to assisted reproductive technologies in animals or humans, and pollen tablets are not relevant to this process.

Pollen tablets are not typically used directly in breeding programs. Breeding programs often involve controlled pollination, hybridization, and selection of desirable traits, but pollen tablets are not a common tool for these processes.

The correct option. Pollen tablets are often marketed as health supplements. They may contain nutrients, vitamins, and minerals derived from pollen and are consumed as a dietary supplement to potentially provide health benefits.

Ex-situ conservation involves maintaining and conserving plant or animal species outside their natural habitat. While pollen may play a role in conserving genetic diversity, pollen tablets are not a standard method for ex-situ conservation.

questions.explanation: Pollen grains are believed to be rich in nutrients (protein 7-­26 % carbohydrates 24-­48%, fats 0.9­-14.5%). They are taken as tablets or syrups to improve health. They also enhance performance of athletes and race horses.

questions.text: The function of filiform apparatus is to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Recognize the suitable pollen at the stigma
options.explanation: The filiform apparatus is a structure found on the surface of the ovule's synergid cells. Its main function is to recognize and interact with the pollen tube, helping guide it toward the ovule for fertilization.
The filiform apparatus is not involved in stimulating the division of the generative cell. The generative cell is part of the pollen grain and undergoes division to form sperm cells.
Nectar production is typically associated with nectaries, which are specialized structures that secrete nectar. The filiform apparatus is not involved in nectar production.

options.text: Guide the entry of the pollen tube
options.explanation: This is the correct function. The filiform apparatus guides the entry of the pollen tube into the ovule, facilitating the process of fertilization.

questions.explanation: In the ovule, the pollen tube is attracted by secretions of synergids. Usually, the pollen tube enters the embryo sac by passing into one of the two synergids and is guided by the filiform apparatus of the synergids in their movement. The pollen tube then breaks open and releases its contents into the embryo sac. Antipodals and synergids later degenerate.

questions.text: Non­-albuminous seed is produced in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is incorrect option.
This option is incorrect.
This option is incorrect.
Non-albuminous seed is produced in pea.

questions.explanation: In majority of dicot seeds,including pea,the endosperm is consumed during seed development and the food is stored in cotyledons and other regions.They are called non­ endospermic or exalbuminous seeds.

questions.text: Meiosis takes place in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Gemmules are a type of asexual reproductive structure found in sponges. Meiosis does not occur in gemmules; instead, gemmules are involved in asexual reproduction.
Meiosis occurs in the formation of megaspores. Megaspores are produced by the megasporocyte through meiosis and develop into the female gametophyte in plants.
A meiocyte is a cell that undergoes meiosis to produce gametes. Therefore, meiosis indeed takes place in a meiocyte.
Conidia are asexual spores produced by fungi, and meiosis does not occur during their formation.

questions.explanation: Gemmule and conidia are asexual propagules thus no meiosis takes place in them. Megaspores are haploid which are formed as a result of meiosis of diploid megaspore mother cell. A meiocyte is any cell that undergoes meiosis.

questions.text: Perisperm differs from endosperm in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Perisperm is generally a diploid tissue, whereas endosperm is typically triploid. The endosperm is formed by the triple fusion of a male gamete with two polar nuclei in the embryo sac.

options.text: Its formation by fusion of secondary nucleus with several sperms
options.explanation: This describes the formation of endosperm, not perisperm. Endosperm results from the triple fusion of one male gamete with two polar nuclei.
Perisperm is not typically haploid. It is usually diploid, originating from the nucellus or integuments.
Both perisperm and endosperm are involved in storing reserve food for the developing embryo. They differ in their origin and ploidy.

questions.explanation: Both perisperm and endosperm are nutritive layers. Perisperm is the residual persistent nucellus of seed prior to fertilization while endosperm develops when one of the sperm cells fuses with two haploid polar nuclei. Thus perisperm is diploid while endosperm is a triploid tissue.

questions.text: The advantage of cleistogamy is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cleistogamy allows plants to produce seeds without relying on external pollinators. The flowers remain closed, and self-pollination occurs, ensuring reproductive success even in the absence of pollinators.
Vivipary refers to the germination of seeds while still attached to the parent plant. Cleistogamy is not directly associated with vivipary.

Cleistogamy tends to result in lower genetic variability because it involves self-pollination. Offspring produced through cleistogamous flowers are more likely to be genetically similar to the parent plant.
While cleistogamy ensures reproductive success, the resulting offspring may not necessarily be more vigorous. The lack of genetic variability may reduce the vigor of the population over time.

questions.explanation: Cleistogamy is the process of pollination and fertilization before the flower has opened. In such flowers, the anther and stigma lie close to each other. When anthers dehisce in the flower buds, pollen grains come in contact with the stigma to effect pollination. Thus, cleistogamous flowers are invariably autogamous as there is no chance of cross­pollen landing on the stigma. Cleistogamous flowers produce assured seed­set even in the absence of pollinators.

questions.text: Megasporangium is equivalent to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The nucellus is a part of the ovule; it surrounds and protects the megasporangium. While it is associated with the ovule, it is not equivalent to the megasporangium itself.

The correct option. The megasporangium is equivalent to the ovule. The megasporangium contains the megaspore, which gives rise to the female gametophyte (embryo sac) within the ovule. The ovule is the structure that eventually develops into a seed after fertilization.

The embryo sac is the female gametophyte of a flowering plant. It is formed within the ovule and contains the egg cells and other cells necessary for fertilization. It is not equivalent to the megasporangium.
The fruit is the mature ovary of a flowering plant, typically containing seeds. It develops from the fertilized ovule after pollination and fertilization. It is not equivalent to the megasporangium.

questions.explanation: In angiosperms, the body of the ovule consists of a mass of parenchymatous cells called nucellus, which is equivalent to megasporangium. A megasporangium along with its protective integuments is called an ovule.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The endothecium is a layer in the anther that helps in dehiscence (opening) and does not produce microspores. Microspores are produced within the microsporangia of the anther.
The tapetum is a nutritive tissue surrounding the developing pollen grains in the anther. It provides essential nutrients to support the maturation and development of pollen.

options.text: Hard outer layer of pollen is called intine
options.explanation: The hard outer layer of pollen is called exine, not intine. The intine is the inner layer of the pollen wall.
The sporogenous tissue, which gives rise to spores, is usually diploid. Meiosis occurs in sporogenous tissue to produce haploid spores.

questions.explanation: A microsporangium is generally surrounded by four wall layers – the epidermis, endothecium, middle layers and the tapetum. The outer three wall layers perfom the function of protection and help in dehiscence of anther to release the pollen. The innermost wall layer is the tapetum. It nourishes the developing pollen grains. Cells of the tapetum are food rich and possess dense cytoplasm and generally have more than one nucleus. They disintegrate to liberate the contents which is absorbed by the developing spores.

questions.text: The product of sexual reproduction generally generates:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: New genetic combination leading to variation
options.explanation: Sexual reproduction involves the fusion of gametes from different individuals, leading to the creation of genetically diverse offspring. This genetic diversity results from the recombination of genetic material during processes like meiosis, contributing to variation within a population.

The size of the organism or the biomass is not directly linked to sexual reproduction. The size of the offspring is influenced by various factors, and sexual reproduction does not necessarily result in larger biomass.
The viability of seeds is not necessarily determined by the mode of reproduction. It depends on factors such as seed physiology, environmental conditions, and seed storage.

The dormancy period of seeds is influenced by various factors, and sexual reproduction itself does not dictate the length of dormancy. Seed dormancy is a complex trait regulated by genetic and environmental factors.

questions.explanation: Sexual reproduction always involves meiosis (zygotic in case of haploid individuals and gametic in case of diploid individuals). Meiosis results in crossing over between chromosomes during prophase-I generating new recombinations. Besides, sexual reproduction generally involves combination of genes from two different organisms. Thus, sexual reproduction generates new genetic combinations leading to variations

questions.text: Animal vectors are required for pollination in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Vallisneria is a water-pollinated plant. Pollination occurs through water currents, and animal vectors are not involved in this process.
Mulberry is generally wind-pollinated. It does not require animal vectors for pollination.
Cucumber flowers are pollinated by animal vectors, especially by bees. Bees transfer pollen between cucumber flowers, aiding in the pollination process.
Maize is anemophilous, meaning it is wind-pollinated. It does not rely on animal vectors for pollination.

questions.explanation: In Vallisneria, water pollination occurs while mulberry and maize undergo wind pollination. In cucumber, animal pollination is observed.

questions.text: Albuminous seeds store their reserve food mainly in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Albuminous seeds primarily store their reserve food in the endosperm, which is a tissue that surrounds the developing embryo in the seed. The endosperm provides nutrients for the germinating seedling.
Cotyledons are the seed leaves, and in non-albuminous seeds, they are the main storage site for reserve food. In albuminous seeds, however, the endosperm serves this purpose.
The hypocotyl is the embryonic region between the cotyledons and the radicle. It is involved in the early growth of the seedling but is not a storage site for reserve food.
Perisperm is a residual tissue in certain seeds that persists from the nucellus. It is not the primary storage site for reserve food in albuminous seeds.

questions.explanation: In some seeds, the endosperm persists in the seed as food storage tissue.Such seeds are called endospermic or albuminous, e.g.,castor, maize, wheat,barley,rubber,coconut.

questions.text: Megaspores are produced from the megaspore mother cells after:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Mitotic division occurs after the formation of megaspores during the development of the female gametophyte (embryo sac), leading to the production of cells with different functions.
The thick wall is typically associated with the formation of spores, including megaspores, but the actual production of megaspores occurs through meiotic division.

Differentiation refers to the process by which cells become specialized into specific cell types. While differentiation is a part of the overall process, the initial production of megaspores involves meiotic division.
Megaspores are produced through the meiotic division of the megaspore mother cell. Meiosis reduces the chromosome number by half, resulting in the formation of haploid megaspores.

questions.explanation: In angiosperms, microsporogenesis i.e., formation of microspores (or pollen grains) occurs by the meiotic divisions of diploid microspore mother cells (or pollen mother cells ). Microsporogenesis takes place in the anther. Megasporogenesis i.e. formation of megaspores occurs by the meiotic divisions of diploid megaspore mother cells. Megasporogenesis takes place in the ovule.

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Cleistogamous flowers are self-pollinating and can be either autogamous (self-fertilization) or facilitated by various mechanisms.

options.text: Xenogamy occurs only by wind pollination
options.explanation: Xenogamy involves the transfer of pollen from one flower to the stigma of a different flower. While wind pollination is one method of achieving xenogamy, other mechanisms like insect pollination can also contribute.

options.text: Chasmogamous flowers do not open at all
options.explanation: Chasmogamous flowers are open flowers that allow for cross-pollination, as opposed to cleistogamous flowers that remain closed during pollination.

options.text: Geitonogamy involves the pollen and stigma of flowers of different plants
options.explanation: Geitonogamy is a form of pollination where pollen is transferred from one flower to the stigma of another flower on the same or a genetically similar plant. It does not involve flowers of different plants.

questions.explanation: In cleistogamy, as the flowers never open so there is no alternative of self pollination. It is invariably autogamous. In xenogamy, pollination takes between two flowers of different plants (genetically and ecologically). It can ocur by wind, water, insects and animals. Chasmogamy occurs when the flowers expose their mature anther and stigma to the pollinating agents. Geitonogamy is the pollination taking place between the two flowers of the same plant or genetically similar plant. Genetically, it is self pollination but as the agency is involved it is ecologically cross pollination.

questions.text: Which of the following statements is correct?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Sporopollenin can be degraded by enzymes
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Sporopollenin is highly resistant to degradation by enzymes, which makes it a durable and long-lasting polymer.

options.text: Sporopollenin is made up of inorganic materials
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Sporopollenin is an organic polymer found in the walls of spores and pollen grains.

options.text: Sporopollenin can withstand high temperatures as well as strong acids and alkalis
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Sporopollenin is known for its resistance to harsh environmental conditions, including high temperatures, strong acids, and alkalis.

options.text: Sporopollenin can withstand high temperatures but not strong acids
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Sporopollenin is resistant to both high temperatures and strong acids, making it a robust and protective material for spores and pollen.

questions.explanation: Pollen grain is a haploid, unicellular body. It is cuticularised and the cutin is of special type called sporopollenin, which is resistant to chemical and biological decomposition. It can withstand high temperatures as well as strong acids and alkalis. This is why, pollen wall is preserved for long periods in fossil deposits. In addition pollen wall possesses proteins for enzymatic and compatability reactions

questions.text: Both autogamy and geitonogamy are prevented in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Papaya is dioecious i.e. male and female occur in a separate plant so that it prevents both autogamy and geitonogamy (methods of self-pollination).

Castor, maize, and cucumber have unisexual flowers but, they are monoecious (male and female flowers are present in the same plant). Hence autogamy will not occur in these plants but, geitonogamy can occur.

Castor, maize, and cucumber have unisexual flowers but, they are monoecious (male and female flowers are present in the same plant). Hence autogamy will not occur in these plants but, geitonogamy can occur.

Castor, maize, and cucumber have unisexual flowers but, they are monoecious (male and female flowers are present in the same plant). Hence autogamy will not occur in these plants but, geitonogamy can occur.

questions.explanation: Papaya is dioecious i.e. male and female occur in a separate plant so that it prevents both autogamy and geitonogamy (methods of self-pollination). Castor, maize, and cucumber have unisexual flowers but, they are monoecious (male and female flowers are present in the same plant). Hence autogamy will not occur in these plants but, geitonogamy can occur.

questions.text: An organic substance that can withstand environmental extremes and cannot be degraded by any enzyme is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The cuticle is a protective waxy layer on the surface of plant organs, and while it provides some resistance to environmental stress, it can be broken down by certain enzymes.
Sporopollenin is a durable organic polymer found in the walls of spores and pollen grains. It is known for its exceptional resistance to environmental extremes and is not easily degraded by enzymes.
Lignin is a complex organic polymer that provides structural support to plant cell walls. While it is resistant to decay, certain microbes and fungi can break it down over time.
Cellulose is a major component of plant cell walls, and while it provides strength and rigidity, it can be broken down by specific enzymes, such as cellulases produced by certain organisms.

questions.explanation: Sporopollenin is a major component of the tough outer (exine) walls of spores and pollen grains. It is chemically very stable and is usually well preserved in soils and sediments. It can withstand environmental extremes and cannot be degraded by enzymes and strong chemical reagents.

questions.text: Which one of the following is correctly matched?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: This is a correct match. Onions are perennials that store energy in a bulb, which is an underground storage organ. The bulb contains layers of modified leaves and serves as a food reserve for the plant.
This is not a correct match. Ginger is typically propagated from rhizomes, not suckers. Rhizomes are underground stems from which new plants grow.
This is not a correct match. Chlamydomonas is a unicellular green alga that reproduces through methods like binary fission, not conidia. Conidia are a type of asexual spore produced by some fungi.

This is not a correct match. Yeast reproduces through budding, where a small daughter cell forms on the parent yeast cell's surface. Zoospores are typically associated with certain types of algae and fungi but not yeast.

questions.explanation: Yeast and other ascomycetes characteristically produce ascospores. Chlamydomonas is an alga and conidia are not found in algae. Ginger propagates by rhizome not by the sucker. Onion propagates by bulb which is an underground, modified stem.

questions.text: Even in absence of pollinating agents, seed­ setting is assured in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Commelina is a genus of plants that includes certain species capable of self-pollination or autonomous self-fertilization.
Zostera refers to a genus of seagrasses, and they are not typically known for autonomous self-pollination.

Salvia is a large genus that includes various species with different pollination mechanisms. While some Salvia species are capable of self-pollination, not all exhibit this trait. The reliance on self-pollination varies among different species within the genus.

Figs (Ficus carica) are known for a unique pollination process involving specialized wasps. The wasps are essential for the development of the fig fruit. While some varieties of figs can develop fruit without the presence of wasps, the overall process and success of seed setting may be impacted.

questions.explanation: plants such as Viola (common pansy), Oxalis, and Commelina produce two types of flowers­ chasmogamous flowers which are similar to flowers of other species with exposed anthers and stigma, and cleistogamous flowers which do not open at all. In such flowers, the anthers and stigma lie close to each other. When anthers dehisce in the flower buds, pollen grains come in contact with the stigma to effect pollination. Thus, cleistogamous flowers are invariably autogamous as there is no chance of cross­pollen landing on the stigma. Cleistogamous flowers produce assured seed­set even in the absence of pollinators.

questions.text: What is the function of germ pore?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The emergence of the radicle, which is the embryonic root, typically occurs during seed germination but is not associated with the germ pore. The radicle emerges from the seed coat to initiate the growth of the young plant.

options.text: Absorption of water for seed germination
options.explanation: Water absorption by the seed is crucial for seed germination, but it is not a function of the germ pore. Water is typically absorbed through the micropyle, another opening in the seed coat.
The initiation of the pollen tube occurs during the process of pollination in flowering plants. It is not related to the germ pore, which is typically associated with seeds.

This is the correct function of the germ pore. In certain types of pollen grains, the germ pore is the structure through which the male gametes (sperm cells) are released when the pollen grain reaches the female reproductive structures of a plant during fertilization.

questions.explanation: In a pollen grain, exine is thin or absent at certain places. These areas may have thickened intine or deposition of callose. They are called germ pores (if rounded) or germinal furrows (if elongated). After pollination, the pollen grain on the stigma absorbs water and nutrients from the stigmatic secretion through its germ pores. The tube or vegetative cell enlarges and comes out of pollen grains through germ pore to form a pollen tube

questions.text: Which one of the following statements is wrong?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: When pollen is shed at the two-­celled stage, double fertilization does not take place
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Double fertilization typically involves the fusion of one sperm cell with the egg cell and the other sperm cell with two polar nuclei, resulting in the formation of both the embryo and endosperm. If pollen is shed at the two-celled stage, the sperm cells are not yet differentiated for this process.

options.text: Vegetative cell is larger than generative cell
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. In pollen grains, the generative cell is typically larger than the vegetative cell. The generative cell gives rise to the sperm cells involved in fertilization.

options.text: Pollen grains in some plants remain viable for months
options.explanation: This statement is correct. Some plants produce pollen grains with protective features, allowing them to remain viable for an extended period, and promoting successful pollination.

options.text: Intine is made up of cellulose and pectin
options.explanation: This statement is correct. The intine is the inner layer of the pollen wall and is composed of cellulose and pectin, among other substances.

questions.explanation: In 60% of flowering plants, the pollen grains are shed at two­celled stage (tube cell + generative cell). Further, development of male gametophyte (pollen grain) occurs on stigma. Pollen grain gives rise to pollen tube which absorbs nourishment from the cells of style for its growth. Generative cell divides to give rise to two male gametes. Out of these, one fuses with the egg to form diploid zygote (generative fertilization or syngamy) whereas the second male gamete fuses with the two haploid polar nuclei or diploid secondary nucleus of the central cell to form primary endosperm nucleus (vegetative fertilization or triple fusion). These two acts of fertilization occur in the same embryo sac and are referred to as double fertilization.

questions.text: Plants with ovaries having only one or a few ovules are generally pollinated by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Bees are effective pollinators for plants with a limited number of ovules. They are attracted to brightly colored flowers, collect nectar, and, in the process, transfer pollen between flowers, promoting cross-pollination.

Butterflies are less efficient pollinators for plants with few ovules compared to bees. They are attracted to colorful flowers but are less consistent in their pollination activities, making them less effective than bees.

Birds, especially hummingbirds, are typically associated with pollinating plants with bright, tubular flowers. While they can be effective pollinators for specific plants, they are not as common as bees in pollinating plants with only one or a few ovules.

Wind pollination is more commonly associated with plants that produce large quantities of pollen and have a high number of ovules, as wind dispersal allows for a greater chance of successful pollination. Plants with few ovules are less likely to rely on wind pollination.

questions.explanation: Anemophily is an abiotic means of pollination by wind and, being non­directional, a wasteful process as the pollen would reach the stigma through wind is a hit­or­miss affair. During the transit of pollen through wind, a considerable amount of pollen is lost because it never reaches a proper stigma. To stand this loss, anemophilous plants have to produce enormous quantities of pollen. Anemophily is also associated with reduction in the number of ovules per ovary. Some models predict that plants benefit from numerous inexpensive flowers distributed throughout the inflorescence, each with a single ovule or a few ovules. In grasses there is just one ovule per ovary. This is to increase the probability of successful pollination of each ovule

questions.text: Filiform apparatus is a characteristic feature of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The suspensor is a structure in the early stages of plant embryo development that provides support to the developing embryo. It does not possess a filiform apparatus.
The egg is the female gamete or ovum. It is involved in fertilization but does not have a filiform apparatus.

The synergid cells are part of the female gametophyte in the ovule of a flowering plant. The filiform apparatus is a characteristic feature of synergid cells and is involved in guiding pollen tubes during fertilization.
The zygote is the result of the fusion of sperm and egg during fertilization. It develops into the embryo. It does not have a filiform apparatus.

questions.explanation: Filliform apparatus is a mass of finger like projections of the wall into the cytoplasm. It is present in synergids (help cells) of the embryo sac, in the micropylar region. It guards the pollen tube inside the ovule towards the embryo sac.

questions.text: Nucellar polyembryony is reported in species of:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Citrus plants, such as oranges and lemons, exhibit nucellar polyembryony. This phenomenon involves the development of multiple embryos from cells of the nucellus (tissue surrounding the embryo sac) instead of the fertilized egg.
Gossypium refers to cotton plants, and they do not commonly exhibit nucellar polyembryony.
Triticum includes wheat species, and nucellar polyembryony is not a typical characteristic of wheat.
Brassica includes plants like cabbage and mustard, and they do not commonly display nucellar polyembryony.

questions.explanation: In nucellar polyembryony, some of the nucellar cells surrounding the embryo sac start dividing. Then it protrudes into the embryo sac and develop into the embryos. In such species, each ovule contains many embryos. Occurrence of more than one embryo in a seed is referred as polyembryony. Nucellar polyembryony is found in many of the Citrus and mango varieties

questions.text: Which one of the following pollinations is autogamous?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Geitonogamy is a type of pollination where pollen is transferred from one flower to another on the same plant, but it involves different flowers. It is not autogamous because it still requires cross-pollination between flowers on the same plant.
Xenogamy is cross-pollination between two different plants of the same species. It is not autogamous because it involves the transfer of pollen between different plants.

Chasmogamy refers to the opening of flowers and exposure of reproductive organs to facilitate cross-pollination by external agents like insects or wind. It is not autogamous because it relies on external agents for pollination.
Cleistogamy is a type of autogamous pollination where a flower remains closed and self-pollinates without the need for external agents like insects or wind.

questions.explanation: Cleistogamy is a type of autogamous pollination where a flower remains closed and self-pollinates without the need for external agents like insects or wind.

questions.text: The “eyes” of the potato tuber are:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Root buds are buds that develop into roots. They are responsible for the formation of new root structures in plants.
Flower buds are buds that develop into flowers. They contain the embryonic tissues that will give rise to the various parts of a flower.
Shoot buds are buds that develop into new shoots or stems. They are responsible for the growth of above-ground parts of the plant.
Axillary buds are located in the axil of a leaf, which is the angle between the leaf and the stem. These buds have the potential to develop into branches, flowers, or new shoots.

questions.explanation: The "eyes" of a potato tuber are actually small, dormant shoots or buds. These buds have the potential to grow into new potato plants when planted in suitable conditions. When you plant a potato tuber with these eyes, these buds will develop into shoots, and eventually, new potato plants will grow from them.

questions.text: Wind pollination is common in:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Legumes are typically pollinated by insects, especially bees, as they offer nectar and have more conspicuous flowers.
Lilies are often pollinated by insects, particularly butterflies, and bees, because they have showy and fragrant flowers that attract these pollinators.

Wind pollination is common in grasses because they typically have small, inconspicuous flowers that lack showy petals and nectar to attract pollinators. Instead, grasses rely on the wind to carry their lightweight pollen from one flower to another.
Orchids use a variety of pollination mechanisms, including insects, birds, and even deception to attract pollinators. They are not primarily wind-pollinated like grasses.

questions.explanation: Wind pollination is common in grasses because they typically have small, inconspicuous flowers that lack showy petals and nectar to attract pollinators. Instead, grasses rely on the wind to carry their lightweight pollen from one flower to another.

questions.text: In angiosperms, functional megaspore develops into:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In angiosperms, the functional megaspore develops into the embryo sac, which is a structure within the ovule that contains the female gametophyte (egg cells).

The ovule is a part of the plant's reproductive structure, and it contains the megasporangium, where the functional megaspore develops into the embryo sac. So, the ovule is related to the development of the embryo sac, but it is not the direct product of the megaspore.

The endosperm is a nutritive tissue that forms after fertilization in angiosperms. It develops from the fusion of a sperm cell with two polar nuclei. It is not derived directly from the functional megaspore.

Pollen sacs are part of the male reproductive structures in angiosperms, and they produce pollen grains, which contain male gametophytes. They are not related to the development of the functional megaspore or embryo sac, which are part of the female reproductive structures.

questions.explanation: In angiosperms, the functional megaspore develops into the embryo sac, which is a structure within the ovule that contains the female gametophyte (egg cells).

questions.text: What is common between vegetative reproduction and apomixis?
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Both are applicable to only dicot plants
options.explanation: This statement is incorrect. Both vegetative reproduction and apomixis can occur in both dicot and monocot plants, so it's not limited to dicots.

While it's true that both vegetative reproduction and apomixis can bypass the traditional flowering and fertilization process, it's not a defining common feature between them. Vegetative reproduction involves the growth of new plants from vegetative parts (e.g., stems, roots), while apomixis specifically refers to the formation of seeds without fertilization.

This statement is not necessarily true for either vegetative reproduction or apomixis. The occurrence of these processes can be influenced by various factors, including plant species, environmental conditions, and cultivation practices. They may not necessarily occur year-round in all cases.

options.text: Both produce progeny identical to the parent
options.explanation: Both vegetative reproduction and apomixis result in offspring that are genetically identical (clones) to the parent plant. In vegetative reproduction, parts of the parent plant, such as stems or roots, give rise to new plants with the same genetic makeup. In apomixis, seeds are produced without fertilization, leading to offspring that are genetic duplicates of the mother plant.

questions.explanation: Both vegetative reproduction and apomixis result in offspring that are genetically identical (clones) to the parent plant. In vegetative reproduction, parts of the parent plant, such as stems or roots, give rise to new plants with the same genetic makeup. In apomixis, seeds are produced without fertilization, leading to offspring that are genetic duplicates of the mother plant.

questions.text: Apomictic embryos in citrus arise from:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Synergids are part of the female gametophyte and are involved in the fertilization process. They do not give rise to apomictic embryos.

Apomictic embryos in Citrus arise from maternal sporophytic tissue in the ovule. Apomixis is a mode of asexual reproduction in plants where embryos are formed without fertilization. In Citrus, apomictic embryos typically develop from cells of the maternal sporophytic tissue within the ovule.
Antipodal cells are also part of the female gametophyte and are not involved in the formation of apomictic embryos.
Apomictic embryos are typically formed without fertilization, so they do not originate from diploid eggs.

questions.explanation: Apomictic embryos in Citrus arise from maternal sporophytic tissue in the ovule. Apomixis is a mode of asexual reproduction in plants where embryos are formed without fertilization. In Citrus, apomictic embryos typically develop from cells of the maternal sporophytic tissue within the ovule.

questions.text: Transfer of pollen grains from the anther to the stigma of another flower of the same plant is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Xenogamy is the transfer of pollen from one plant to the stigma of a flower on a different plant. It is not the same as transferring pollen between flowers on the same plant.
Geitonogamy is the transfer of pollen grains from the anther of one flower to the stigma of another flower on the same plant. This is a form of pollination that occurs within the same plant.
Karyogamy is the fusion of nuclei during sexual reproduction, typically after fertilization. It is not related to the transfer of pollen between flowers.

Autogamy is self-pollination, where pollen from the anther of one flower fertilizes the stigma of the same flower. It does not involve the transfer of pollen between different flowers on the same plant.
