options.text: Proliferative phase: Rapid regeneration of myometrium and maturation of Graffian follicle
options.explanation: The proliferative phase is the phase of the menstrual cycle characterized by the rapid regeneration of the endometrium. During this phase, the endometrial lining thickens and prepares for potential embryo implantation. Simultaneously, in the ovaries, the Graffian follicle (a mature ovarian follicle) is developing and maturing.

options.text: Ovulation : LH and FSH attain peak level and sharp fall in the secretion of progesterone
options.explanation: Ovulation is the release of a mature ovum (egg) from the ovary. During ovulation, there is a surge in the secretion of luteinizing hormone (LH) and follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) from the anterior pituitary gland. The sharp rise in LH triggers the release of the mature egg from the ovary.

options.text: Secretory phase : Development of corpus luteurn and increased secretion of progesterone
options.explanation: The secretory phase is the phase of the menstrual cycle that follows ovulation. After ovulation, the empty ovarian follicle becomes the corpus luteum, which secretes progesterone. The secretory phase is characterized by increased secretion of progesterone to prepare the endometrium for embryo implantation.

questions.explanation: The secretory phase is the phase of the menstrual cycle that follows ovulation. After ovulation, the empty ovarian follicle becomes the corpus luteum, which secretes progesterone. questions.text: The mammalian corpus luteum produces:

options.explanation: Estrogen is a female sex hormone produced primarily by the developing ovarian follicles. It plays a crucial role in the development of female secondary sexual characteristics, regulation of the menstrual cycle, and preparation of the uterine lining for potential embryo implantation. While the corpus luteum does produce some estrogen, its primary hormone of production is progesterone.

The mammalian corpus luteum is a temporary endocrine structure that forms from the collapsed ovarian follicle after ovulation. Its main function is to secrete progesterone. Progesterone plays a critical role in maintaining the endometrial lining (uterine wall) during the secretory phase of the menstrual cycle, creating an environment for embryo implantation. If fertilization occurs, the corpus luteum will continue to produce progesterone to support early pregnancy until the placenta can take over hormone production.

Luteinizing hormone (LH) is a hormone produced by the anterior pituitary gland. It plays a crucial role in the menstrual cycle by triggering ovulation. During the mid-cycle surge of LH, the mature ovarian follicle ruptures, releasing the mature egg (secondary oocyte) from the ovary. After ovulation, the remaining follicle cells form the corpus luteum, which then produces progesterone.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Estrogen is one of the primary female sex hormones produced by the developing ovarian follicles. It plays a crucial role in the regulation of the menstrual cycle and the development of female secondary sexual characteristics. Estrogen is responsible for stimulating the growth and thickening of the endometrial lining (uterine wall) during the proliferative phase of the menstrual cycle. However, withdrawal of estrogen is not the immediate cause of menstruation.

FSH-RH is a hormone produced by the hypothalamus that stimulates the release of follicle-stimulating hormone (FSH) from the anterior pituitary gland. FSH, in turn, stimulates the growth and development of ovarian follicles, including the Graafian follicle that contains the mature ovum. However, FSH-RH is not directly involved in the immediate cause of menstruation.

FSH is a hormone produced by the anterior pituitary gland. As mentioned earlier, FSH stimulates the growth and development of ovarian follicles. While FSH levels do fluctuate during the menstrual cycle, its withdrawal is not the immediate cause of menstruation.

Progesterone is a hormone primarily produced by the corpus luteum, a temporary endocrine structure formed from the collapsed ovarian follicle after ovulation. Progesterone plays a crucial role in maintaining the endometrial lining (uterine wall) during the secretory phase of the menstrual cycle, creating an environment conducive for embryo implantation. If fertilization does not occur, the corpus luteum regresses, leading to a withdrawal of progesterone. The withdrawal of progesterone causes the endometrial lining to break down, leading to menstruation.

questions.explanation: Based on the explanations provided and the image, the correct answer to the question is D) Progesterone. The withdrawal of progesterone is the immediate cause of menstruation.

questions.text: In human female, ovulation occurs during menstrual cycle:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: At the end of proliferative phase
options.explanation: Ovulation in human females typically occurs at the end of the proliferative phase of the menstrual cycle. The proliferative phase is also known as the follicular phase, and it is the phase in which the ovarian follicles, containing the immature eggs (oocytes), begin to develop and mature. As the follicles mature, there is an increase in estrogen production, which stimulates the thickening of the endometrial lining (uterine wall) in preparation for potential embryo implantation. Ovulation usually takes place around the 14th to 16th day of a regular 28-day menstrual cycle.

options.text: In the middle of secretory phase
options.explanation: The secretory phase occurs after ovulation and is characterized by the development of the corpus luteum and increased production of progesterone, which prepares the endometrial lining for potential pregnancy. Ovulation does not occur during the secretory phase.

options.text: Just before the end of secretory phase
options.explanation: Ovulation occurs at the end of the proliferative phase, not during the secretory phase.

options.text: In the beginning of proliferative phase
options.explanation: Ovulation does not occur at the beginning of the proliferative phase. It occurs towards the end of this phase when the ovarian follicles have matured and are ready for release.

questions.explanation: Based on the explanations provided and the image, the correct answer to the question is A) At the end of the proliferative phase. Ovulation in human females usually occurs at the end of the proliferative phase, when the ovarian follicles have matured and the endometrial lining is thickened for potential embryo implantation.

questions.text: Secretion of progesterone by corpus luteum is initiated by:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: MSH is a hormone primarily involved in the regulation of skin pigmentation. It is not directly involved in the initiation of progesterone secretion by the corpus luteum.

LH plays a crucial role in initiating the secretion of progesterone by the corpus luteum. After ovulation, the collapsed ovarian follicle transforms into the corpus luteum, which is a temporary endocrine structure. LH stimulates the corpus luteum to produce and secrete progesterone. Progesterone is essential for preparing the endometrial lining (uterine wall) for potential embryo implantation and maintaining a supportive environment for early pregnancy.
Testosterone is a male sex hormone and is not involved in the initiation of progesterone secretion by the corpus luteum.
Thyroxine, also known as T4, is a hormone produced by the thyroid gland that regulates metabolism. It is not directly involved in the initiation of progesterone secretion by the corpus luteum.

questions.explanation: Based on the explanations provided, the correct answer to the question is B) LH. LH initiates the secretion of progesterone by the corpus luteum.

questions.text: Correct sequence of hormone secretion from beginning of menstruation is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: In the beginning of menstruation, the hormonal sequence typically starts with a rise in FSH (Follicle-Stimulating Hormone) levels. FSH stimulates the growth and maturation of ovarian follicles, which contain immature eggs (oocytes). As the follicles develop, they produce increasing amounts of estrogen. Later in the menstrual cycle, after ovulation, the collapsed ovarian follicle transforms into the corpus luteum, which secretes progesterone. Therefore, the correct sequence is FSH, estrogen, and then progesterone.

This sequence is not correct. As mentioned earlier, FSH is secreted first, leading to the maturation of follicles and the production of estrogen, which is followed by the secretion of progesterone by the corpus luteum after ovulation.

The hormonal sequence during the menstrual cycle begins with an increase in FSH levels, followed by the production of estrogen as the ovarian follicles mature. After ovulation, the corpus luteum secretes progesterone.
This sequence is not correct. FSH is the first hormone to rise at the beginning of menstruation, leading to the production of estrogen and later, the secretion of progesterone.

questions.explanation: The hormonal sequence during the menstrual cycle begins with an increase in FSH levels, followed by the production of estrogen as the ovarian follicles mature. After ovulation, the corpus luteum secretes progesterone.

questions.text: One of the following is a haploid cell:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Primordial germ cells are the cells that give rise to gametes (sperm and eggs) during the development of an organism. They are diploid cells because they have two sets of chromosomes.
Oogonium is the diploid germ cell found in the ovaries of females. These cells undergo mitosis to produce primary oocytes.
A primary oocyte is a diploid cell that undergoes meiosis to produce a secondary oocyte and a polar body. It is diploid because it has two sets of chromosomes.

After the first meiotic division of the primary oocyte, it forms a secondary oocyte and a polar body. The secondary oocyte is a haploid cell because it contains only one set of chromosomes (23 chromosomes in humans). It is released from the ovary during ovulation and, if fertilized by a sperm, will complete meiosis to become an ovum.

questions.explanation: After the first meiotic division of the primary oocyte, it forms a secondary oocyte and a polar body. The secondary oocyte is a haploid cell because it contains only one set of chromosomes (23 chromosomes in humans). questions.text: Onset of menstruation of human female is called:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Menopause is a stage in a woman's life when her menstrual periods permanently cease, and she can no longer become pregnant. It typically occurs around the age of 45 to 55 years and is characterized by a decrease in hormone production, particularly estrogen and progesterone. Menopause marks the end of a woman's reproductive years.

Puberty is the period of life during which a person's body undergoes physical changes that lead to sexual maturity. In females, puberty involves the development of secondary sexual characteristics, such as breast development, body hair growth, and the onset of menstruation (menarche). Puberty usually occurs between the ages of 10 and 14 years.
Gestation refers to the period of development of an embryo or fetus within the uterus of a pregnant female. It is the time between conception and birth.

Menarche is the term used to describe the first occurrence of menstruation in a female's life. It is a significant event that signals the onset of reproductive capability and typically occurs during puberty. Menarche usually happens between the ages of 9 and 16 years.

questions.explanation: Menarche is the term used to describe the first occurrence of menstruation in a female's life. It is a significant event that signals the onset of reproductive capability and typically occurs during puberty. Menarche usually happens between the ages of 9 and 16 years. So the correct answer to the question is D) Menarche.

questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Corpora cardiaca is a part of the insect brain, specifically associated with the neurosecretory cells. It is not related to the hormone that prepares and maintains the uterus during the secretory phase in humans.

During the secretory phase of the menstrual cycle, the ruptured follicle in the ovary transforms into a structure called the corpus luteum. The corpus luteum is a temporary endocrine structure that secretes the hormone progesterone. Progesterone helps prepare and maintain the uterine lining (endometrium) for potential embryo implantation and pregnancy.

Corpora albicans are structures that form in the ovary after the degeneration of the corpus luteum. They are not involved in the secretion of hormones that prepare or maintain the uterus during the secretory phase.

The Graafian follicle is a mature ovarian follicle that contains a developing ovum (egg). It plays a role in ovulation but is not involved in secreting hormones that prepare or maintain the uterus during the secretory phase.

questions.explanation: During the secretory phase of the menstrual cycle, the ruptured follicle in the ovary transforms into a structure called the corpus luteum. The corpus luteum is a temporary endocrine structure that secretes the hormone progesterone. Progesterone helps prepare and maintain the uterine lining (endometrium) for potential embryo implantation and pregnancy.

questions.text: Polar bodies are formed during:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Somatic hybridization is a laboratory technique used to create hybrid cells or organisms by fusing two different somatic cells. It is not related to the formation of polar bodies.

Spermatogenesis is the process of producing sperm cells (spermatozoa) in the testes of males. During spermatogenesis, diploid spermatogonial cells undergo meiosis to form haploid sperm cells. It does not involve the formation of polar bodies.

Oogenesis is the process of producing ova (eggs) in the ovaries of females. During oogenesis, diploid oogonial cells undergo meiosis to form haploid eggs. Along with the formation of eggs, polar bodies are also produced. Polar bodies are small, non-functional cells that are generated during meiosis but do not develop into viable eggs. They are a byproduct of the unequal division of cytoplasm during oogenesis.

Spermiogenesis is the final stage of spermatogenesis, where the newly formed haploid spermatids mature into fully functional sperm cells (spermatozoa). It does not involve the formation of polar bodies.

questions.explanation: Oogenesis is the process of producing ova (eggs) in the ovaries of females. During oogenesis, diploid oogonial cells undergo meiosis to form haploid eggs. questions.text: Chromosome number is halved during:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The first polar body is formed during the first meiotic division (Meiosis I) of oogenesis. During this process, a diploid primary oocyte divides into a haploid secondary oocyte and a small non-functional cell called the first polar body. The chromosome number is halved during the formation of the first polar body because it results in the reduction of genetic material from diploid (2n) to haploid (n).

The second polar body is formed during the second meiotic division (Meiosis II) of oogenesis. It is a result of the division of the haploid secondary oocyte. The second polar body is also a small non-functional cell and does not develop into a viable egg. However, it plays a role in further reducing the genetic material in the egg.

Meiosis II is the second phase of meiosis, where the haploid cells resulting from Meiosis I further divide to form gametes (sperm or eggs). In this stage, the chromosome number is not halved; rather, the sister chromatids of each chromosome are separated, leading to the formation of haploid cells.

The division of the secondary oocyte refers to the process of Meiosis II in oogenesis, which leads to the formation of the second polar body and the mature egg (ovum). As mentioned earlier, the chromosome number is halved during the formation of the polar bodies.

questions.explanation: The first polar body is formed during the first meiotic division (Meiosis I) of oogenesis. During this process, a diploid primary oocyte divides into a haploid secondary oocyte and a small non-functional cell called the first polar body. The chromosome number is halved during the formation of the first polar body because it results in the reduction of genetic material from diploid (2n) to haploid (n).

questions.text: After entry of sperm into cytoplasm of ovum which of the following event takes place?

questions.text: The right sequence of stages during menstrual cycle:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Follicular stage --> luteal stage —> ovulation ---> menstrual
options.explanation: The correct order is Follicular --> ovulation --> luteal --> menstruation. Refer to the main explaiantion below.
The correct order is Follicular --> ovulation --> luteal --> menstruation. Refer to the main explaiantion below.

Menstrual phase: This is the first phase of the menstrual cycle, and it begins when the lining of the uterus sheds. This phase typically lasts for 3-5 days. Follicular phase: This phase begins after menstruation and ends with ovulation. During this phase, an egg matures in one of the ovaries. Ovulation: This is the phase in which the egg is released from the ovary. Ovulation typically occurs around day 14 of a 28-day menstrual cycle. Luteal phase: This phase begins after ovulation and ends with the start of the next menstrual period. During this phase, the egg travels down the fallopian tube and implants in the uterus if it is fertilized. If the egg is not fertilized, it will break down and menstruation will begin again. Hence, the correct order is Follicular --> ovulation --> luteal --> menstruation.
The correct order is Follicular --> ovulation --> luteal --> menstruation. Refer to the main explaiantion below.

Ovulation: This is the phase in which the egg is released from the ovary. Ovulation typically occurs around day 14 of a 28-day menstrual cycle. Luteal phase: This phase begins after ovulation and ends with the start of the next menstrual period. During this phase, the egg travels down the fallopian tube and implants in the uterus if it is fertilized. If the egg is not fertilized, it will break down and menstruation will begin again. Hence, the correct order is Follicular --> ovulation --> luteal --> menstruation. So, option C is correct.

questions.text: Breakdown of the endometrium during menstruation is due to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: Increase in the level of LH
options.explanation: LH (luteinizing hormone) surge triggers ovulation during the menstrual cycle. It doesn't directly cause the breakdown of the endometrial lining. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Decrease in the level of progesterone
options.explanation: If fertilization doesn't occur, the corpus luteum starts to degenerate, leading to a decrease in progesterone production. This decrease in progesterone levels triggers a breakdown of the endometrial lining. Without the support of progesterone, the blood vessels in the endometrium constrict, leading to reduced blood flow to the tissue. This causes the superficial layers of the endometrial lining to slough off, resulting in menstruation.

options.text: Increase in the level of FSH
options.explanation: FSH (follicle-stimulating hormone) is responsible for the growth and development of follicles in the ovaries. It's not directly involved in the breakdown of the endometrium. Hence, this option is incorrect.

options.text: Increase in the level of estrogen
options.explanation: Estrogen promotes the growth and thickening of the endometrial lining during the follicular phase of the menstrual cycle. While its levels do play a role in the menstrual cycle, an increase in estrogen alone does not directly cause the breakdown of the endometrium.

questions.explanation: If fertilization doesn't occur, the corpus luteum starts to degenerate, leading to a decrease in progesterone production. This decrease in progesterone levels triggers a breakdown of the endometrial lining. Without the support of progesterone, the blood vessels in the endometrium constrict, leading to reduced blood flow to the tissue. This causes the superficial layers of the endometrial lining to slough off, resulting in menstruation. So option B is correct.

questions.text: Uterus opens into vagina through:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The uterus opens into the vagina through the cervix. The cervix is a cylindrical neck of tissue that connects the uterus to the vagina. During childbirth, the cervix dilates (opens) to allow the baby to pass from the uterus into the vagina and eventually be born.

The fallopian tubes are not connected to the uterus in a way that it opens into the vagina. The fallopian tubes are responsible for transporting eggs from the ovaries to the uterus and are not involved in the pathway from the uterus to the vagina. Hence, this option is incorrect.

questions.text: Main duct of the male reproductive tract is:

options.explanation: The seminiferous tubules are tiny tubes located in the testes. They are responsible for producing sperm. The seminiferous tubules are about 100 feet long in an adult male.

The main duct of the male reproductive tract is the vas deferens, also known as the ductus deferens. It is a long, muscular tube that transports sperm from the epididymis to the ejaculatory duct. The vas deferens is approximately 40 cm long and is the longest duct in the male reproductive tract. In human females, the periodic reproductive cycle is completed in approximately 28 days. This is known as the menstrual cycle. The menstrual cycle is divided into three phases.The follicular phase begins with the first day of menstruation and ends with ovulation. During this phase, an egg cell matures in an ovary. The ovulatory phase occurs about 14 days before the next menstrual period begins. During this phase, the egg cell is released from the ovary. The luteal phase begins after ovulation and ends with the start of the next menstrual period. During this phase, the egg cell travels down the fallopian tube and may be fertilized by a sperm cell. If the egg cell is not fertilized, it will degenerate and the menstrual cycle will begin again.

questions.text: Breakdown of endometrium during menstruation is due to:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Luteinizing hormone (LH) is a hormone that is produced by the pituitary gland. It is responsible for stimulating ovulation, the release of an egg cell from the ovary. An increase in LH levels can cause a number of changes in the body, e.g LH triggers ovulation, the release of an egg cell from the ovary. LH stimulates the production of progesterone by the corpus luteum, the remnant of the ovary that released the egg cell. LH stimulates the growth of the endometrium, the lining of the uterus.

The breakdown of the endometrium during menstruation is due to a decrease in the levels of the hormone progesterone. This hormone is responsible for maintaining the lining of the uterus, and when its levels decrease, the lining begins to break down.

Progesterone is a hormone that is produced by the ovaries. It is responsible for preparing the uterus for pregnancy. An increase in progesterone levels can cause a number of changes in the body, including, changes in the menstrual cycle. Progesterone helps to regulate the menstrual cycle. An increase in progesterone levels can lead to longer menstrual cycles, or to heavier periods. Changes in the breasts e.g progesterone can cause the breasts to become fuller and more tender. It can also lead to the development of breast milk in pregnant women. Changes in the skin e.g Progesterone can cause the skin to become drier and more sensitive. It can also lead to the development of acne. Changes in the mood e.g progesterone can affect the mood, causing some women to feel more emotional or irritable.

A rise in estrogen levels can cause a number of changes in the body, including: Changes in the menstrual cycle: Estrogen helps to regulate the menstrual cycle. An increase in estrogen levels can lead to shorter or longer menstrual cycles, or to heavier or lighter periods. Changes in the breasts, estrogen can cause the breasts to grow and become more sensitive. Changes in the skin, estrogen can cause the skin to become thicker and more oily. It can also lead to the development of acne. Changes in the hair, estrogen can cause the hair to become thicker and darker. It can also lead to the growth of hair in places where it is not normally found, such as the face or chin. Changes in the mood, estrogen can affect the mood, causing some women to feel more emotional or irritable.

questions.text: The carriers may transmit such diseases to their healthy partners:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Organic diseases are caused by problems with the body's organs or tissues. These problems can be caused by a variety of factors, including infection, injury, or environmental exposure. Organic diseases can affect many different parts of the body, and they can range from mild to severe. Some examples of organic diseases include heart disease, cancer, and diabetes.

Genetic diseases are caused by changes in the genes, which are the basic units of inheritance. These changes can be inherited from parents, or they can occur spontaneously during development. Genetic diseases can affect many different parts of the body, and they can range from mild to severe. Some examples of genetic diseases include cystic fibrosis, sickle cell anemia, and Down syndrome.

Sexually transmitted diseases (STDs), also referred to as sexually transmitted infections (STIs), are infections that can be spread through sexual contact. There are many different types of STDs, and they can range from mild to severe. Some STDs, such as chlamydia and gonorrhea, can be easily treated with antibiotics. Other STDs, such as HIV and herpes, can be more difficult to treat and can have serious health consequences.

Congenital diseases are conditions that are present at birth. They can be caused by genetic factors, environmental factors, or a combination of both. Some common congenital diseases include down syndrome which is a genetic disorder that causes intellectual disability and physical characteristics such as a flat facial profile and upward-slanting eyes. Cleft lip and palate are birth defects that occur when the lip or palate does not completely close during development.

questions.explanation: Seminiferous tubules are tiny, coiled tubes found in the testes.They are the site of sperm production.Each testis contains about 800 seminiferous tubules.The tubules are lined with two types of cells: germ cells and Sertoli cells.Germ cells are the cells that give rise to sperm. So A is the correct option.

questions.text: Mature sperms are stored in the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Seminiferous tubules are tiny, coiled tubes found in the testes.They are the site of sperm production.
Mature sperms are stored in the epididymis. The epididymis is a long, coiled tube that is attached to the back of each testis. It is where sperm mature and are stored until they are ejaculated.
Vas deferens are two long, muscular tubes that carry sperm from the epididymis to the urethra.
The seminal vesicles are two small, sac-like glands that are located behind the bladder in men. They produce a fluid that is a major component of semen.

questions.explanation: Mature sperms are stored in the epididymis. The epididymis is a long, coiled tube that is attached to the back of each testis. It is where sperm mature and are stored until they are ejaculated. Hence B is the correct option.

questions.text: When a mature egg leaves the ovary, it enters the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Follicles are sacs in the ovaries that contain the eggs. The eggs mature inside the follicles. When an egg is mature, it is released from the follicle during ovulation.

Interstitial cells are found in the ovaries. They produce testosterone, the primary female sex hormone. Testosterone is responsible for the development of female secondary sex characteristics, such as breasts and pubic hair.

The endometrium is the lining of the uterus. It is made up of a layer of tissue that is rich in blood vessels and glands. The endometrium thickens during the menstrual cycle in preparation for pregnancy. If the egg is not fertilized, the endometrium breaks down and is shed during menstruation.
When a mature egg leaves the ovary, it enters the fallopian tube. The fallopian tubes are two long, thin tubes that extend from the ovaries to the uterus. They are also known as the oviducts.

questions.explanation: When a mature egg leaves the ovary, it enters the fallopian tube. The fallopian tubes are two long, thin tubes that extend from the ovaries to the uterus. They are also known as the oviducts. So correct option is D.

questions.text: The endometrium is the lining of the:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The bladder lining, also known as the urothelium, is a specialized layer of tissue that lines the inside of the bladder. The urothelium is made up of a single layer of cells that are tightly connected together. These cells are specialized to withstand the constant exposure to urine.

The endometrium is the lining of the uterus. It is a layer of tissue that is rich in blood vessels and glands. The endometrium thickens during the menstrual cycle in preparation for pregnancy. If the egg is not fertilized, the endometrium breaks down and is shed during menstruation.

The vagina is the muscular tube that connects the cervix to the outside of the body. It is lined with a thin layer of squamous epithelium, which is made up of flat, non-ciliated cells. The squamous epithelium helps to protect the vagina from infection.

The oviduct, also known as the fallopian tube, is a long, narrow tube that connects the ovary to the uterus. It is lined with a ciliated epithelium, which is made up of cells that have hair-like projections called cilia. The cilia help to move the egg down the oviduct towards the uterus.

questions.explanation: The endometrium is the lining of the uterus. It is a layer of tissue that is rich in blood vessels and glands. The endometrium thickens during the menstrual cycle in preparation for pregnancy. If the egg is not fertilized, the endometrium breaks down and is shed during menstruation. So correct option is B.

questions.text: Spermatozoa are nourished during their development by
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Spermatozoa are nourished during their development by Sertoli cells. Sertoli cells are a type of cell that lines the seminiferous tubules in the testes. They provide nutrients and support to the developing sperm cells.Sertoli cells also produce a fluid that surrounds the sperm cells. This fluid helps to protect the sperm cells from the immune system and helps to keep them motile.

Connective tissue is a type of tissue that binds together other tissues and organs in the body. It is made up of cells, fibers, and ground substance. The cells in connective tissue are responsible for providing support, strength, and elasticity to the tissue.

The intestine is made up of many different types of cells, each with its own unique function. Some of the most important types of intestinal cells include: Enterocytes are the most abundant cells in the intestine. They are responsible for absorbing nutrients from food. Goblet cells produce mucus, which helps to protect the intestine from infection and to keep food moving through the digestive tract.

Mast cells are a type of white blood cell that is found in connective tissue throughout the body. They are responsible for releasing a variety of chemicals, including histamine, serotonin, and heparin, in response to an allergic reaction or injury. These chemicals cause inflammation and swelling, which can lead to symptoms such as itching, redness, and pain.

questions.explanation: Spermatozoa are nourished during their development by sertoli cells. Sertoli cells are a type of cell that lines the seminiferous tubules in the testes. They provide nutrients and support to the developing sperm cells.Sertoli cells also produce a fluid that surrounds the sperm cells. This fluid helps to protect the sperm cells from the immune system and helps to keep them motile. So correct option is A.

questions.text: Number of spermatozoa that a single primary spermatocyte ultimately produces in spermatogenesis is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A single primary spermatocyte ultimately produces 4 spermatozoa in spermatogenesis.This is because during spermatogenesis, a primary spermatocyte undergoes two meiotic divisions, resulting in four haploid spermatids. Each spermatid then undergoes a process called spermiogenesis, during which it matures into a spermatozoon.

A single primary spermatocyte ultimately produces 4 spermatozoa in spermatogenesis.This is because during spermatogenesis, a primary spermatocyte undergoes two meiotic divisions, resulting in four haploid spermatids. Each spermatid then undergoes a process called spermiogenesis, during which it matures into a spermatozoon.

Each spermatid then undergoes a process called spermiogenesis, during which it matures into a spermatozoon.

Each spermatid then undergoes a process called spermiogenesis, during which it matures into a spermatozoon.

questions.explanation: A single primary spermatocyte ultimately produces 4 spermatozoa in spermatogenesis.This is because during spermatogenesis, a primary spermatocyte undergoes two meiotic divisions, resulting in four haploid spermatids. Each spermatid then undergoes a process called spermiogenesis, during which it matures into a spermatozoa. So option B is correct.

questions.text: Which of the following is the largest in size?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: A mature sperm cell is about 5 micrometers in diameter.

options.text: They are all approximately the same size
options.explanation: They all are different sizes.
The mature egg cell is the largest in size among the options given. It is about 120 micrometers in diameter,
Polar bodies are smaller, at about 2 micrometers in diameter.

questions.explanation: The mature egg cell is the largest in size among the options given. It is about 120 micrometers in diameter, while a mature sperm cell is about 5 micrometers in diameter. Polar bodies are even smaller, at about 2 micrometers in diameter. So option C is correct.

questions.text: Menstrual cycle begins with break down of the womb wall. The ovum or egg is released:
questions.subject: Biology

options.text: At the beginning of the cycle
options.explanation: The follicular phase is the first phase of the menstrual cycle. During this phase, the egg follicle in the ovary matures and the levels of estrogen and progesterone in the blood increase.

On day 20 of the menstrual cycle, the woman is in the luteal phase. The luteal phase is the second half of the menstrual cycle, and it begins after ovulation. During the luteal phase, the corpus luteum, the remains of the egg follicle, produces progesterone. Progesterone helps to prepare the uterus for pregnancy.

options.text: About half way through the cycle
options.explanation: The ovum or egg is released about halfway through the menstrual cycle, during ovulation.

options.text: At the end of the cycle
options.explanation: Menstruation is the fourth and final phase of the menstrual cycle. During this phase, the lining of the uterus breaks down and is shed through the vagina.

questions.explanation: The ovum or egg is released about halfway through the menstrual cycle, during ovulation.Ovulation is the process of the egg being released from the ovary. The egg then travels down the fallopian tube towards the uterus. If the egg is fertilized by a sperm, it will implant in the lining of the uterus and pregnancy will occur.If the egg is not fertilized, it will eventually pass out of the body through the vagina. This is called menstruation. So option C is correct.

questions.text: In 28-days human ovarian cycle, ovulation occurs on:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: At day 1, menstruation ends. The estrogen levels begin to rise and the progesterone levels are still low.
Ovulation occurs on day 14 of a 28-day human ovarian cycle. This means that ovulation occurs halfway through the menstrual cycle.
At day 10, estrogen levels is at peak. The progesterone levels begin to rise. Ovulation is likely to occur within the next few days.
At day 28, menstruation begins. This is the shedding of the lining of the uterus. Estrogen levels are low. Progesterone levels are low

questions.explanation: Ovulation occurs on day 14 of a 28-day human ovarian cycle. This means that ovulation occurs halfway through the menstrual cycle.

questions.text: After ovulation endocrine part of ovary is:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The endocrine part of the ovary after ovulation is the corpus luteum. The corpus luteum is a temporary endocrine gland that is formed from the remains of the ruptured ovarian follicle after ovulation. It produces the hormones progesterone and estrogen, which help to prepare the uterus for pregnancy. If the egg is not fertilized, the corpus luteum will eventually break down and the levels of progesterone and estrogen will decrease. This will trigger menstruation, which is the shedding of the lining of the uterus.
The corpus albicans is the regressed form of the corpus luteum.
The corpus callosum is the curved band of lighter tissue at the center of the brain above the hypothalamus.
The corpus spongiosum is the mass of spongy tissue surrounding the male urethra within the penis.

questions.explanation: The endocrine part of the ovary after ovulation is the corpus luteum.The corpus luteum is a temporary endocrine gland that is formed from the remains of the ruptured ovarian follicle after ovulation. It produces the hormones progesterone and estrogen, which help to prepare the uterus for pregnancy. If the egg is not fertilized, the corpus luteum will eventually break down and the levels of progesterone and estrogen will decrease. This will trigger menstruation, which is the shedding of the lining of the uterus.

questions.text: During pregnancy, the production of ova in ovary is prevented by
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Estrogen is a sex hormone that is produced by the ovaries. It is responsible for the development of female secondary sex characteristics, such as breast development and menstruation. Estrogen also plays a role in pregnancy, helping to prepare the uterus for implantation and supporting the development of the fetus.

During pregnancy, the production of ova in the ovary is prevented by the high levels of the hormone progesterone. Progesterone is produced by the corpus luteum, which is a temporary endocrine gland that is formed from the remains of the ruptured ovarian follicle after ovulation. Progesterone helps to maintain pregnancy by preventing the uterus from contracting and expelling the embryo. It also helps to prepare the breasts for lactation.

Relaxin is a hormone that is produced by the placenta during pregnancy. It helps to relax the ligaments in the pelvis, making it easier for the baby to pass through the birth canal. Relaxin also helps to lower blood pressure and improve blood sugar control.

Prolactin is a hormone that is produced by the pituitary gland. It is responsible for milk production in breastfeeding women. Prolactin also plays a role in the menstrual cycle, helping to suppress ovulation.

questions.explanation: During pregnancy, the production of ova in the ovary is prevented by the high levels of the hormone progesterone. Progesterone is produced by the corpus luteum, which is a temporary endocrine gland that is formed from the remains of the ruptured ovarian follicle after ovulation. Progesterone helps to maintain pregnancy by preventing the uterus from contracting and expelling the embryo. It also helps to prepare the breasts for lactation.

questions.text: Menopause is the stage in an female when:
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Menstruation, also known as a period, is the shedding of the lining of the uterus. It is a normal part of the menstrual cycle, which is the monthly cycle of changes that happen in the female reproductive system.The average age for a girl to start menstruating is 12.

options.text: Menstruation stops and reproductive capacity is arrested
options.explanation: Menopause is the stage in a woman's life when her ovaries stop producing eggs. This usually happens between the ages of 45 and 55, but it can happen earlier or later.

Puberty is the time in a person's life when their body starts to change and develop into an adult. It is a natural process that happens to everyone, but it can start at different times for different people. For girls, puberty usually starts between the ages of 8 and 13.

questions.explanation: Menopause is the stage in a woman's life when her ovaries stop producing eggs. This usually happens between the ages of 45 and 55, but it can happen earlier or later. So option B is correct.

questions.text: Which of the following is used in most of the oral contraceptives?
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: LH named as lutineising harmone. It is responsible for the lutenisation of ova. It is also responsible for inducing ovulation.

Androgen harmones are produced from the adrenal glands which are responsible for developing male secondry characters.If are produced in more amount in females, due to tumour, it can lead to more male appearences in females.
Follicle Stimulating Harmone is produced in puberty. It stimulates the follicles in the ovaries to produce ova.
Progesterone is responsible for preventing ovulation. It is used in birth control pills(oral contraceptives).

questions.explanation: Oral contraceptives are used to prevent pregnancy. Progesterone is primarily responsible for preventing pregnancy. So, it is taken as an oral contraceptive and is used in birth control pills. This hormone develops the endometrium and make it receptive for the implantation of the zygote and on the other hand prevents ovulation from ovaries. Progesterone inhibits further FSH secretion from the pituitary, thus preventing any more follicles from ripening. It also afects the uterus, causing further thickening and vascularization of its wall, and other areas of the female body, preparing it for maintaining the state of pregnancy. It suppresses ovulation. That is why it is a major constituent of birth control pill.

questions.text: Number of chromosomes in primary spermatocytes is
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: The term haploid refers to the condition in which organism or a cell has half number of chromosomes than the normal. Primary spermetocytes don't have haploid number of chromosomes.
Triploid means having an extra number of chromosomes than the normal. e.g; one diploid+one haploid
Diploid refers to the normal or 2x number of chromosomes. Primary spermetocyte is a diploid cell.
Polyploid refers to a condition in which a cell or organism has many sets of chromosomes. It is an abnormality and rarely occurs.

questions.explanation: The diploid spermetogonia undergoes mitosis to form diploid primary spermetocytes. Primary spermetocytes undergoes division by meiosis to form 2 haploid secondry spermetocytes.

questions.text: 50 secondary spermatocytes and 50 secondary oocytes will give rise to
questions.subject: Biology

options.explanation: Each secondary spermatocyte gives rise to 2 sperm and each secondary oocyte gives rise to 1 ova and 1 polar body. The polar body is ruptured and is used as a source of nutrition, so only one ova is produced by 1 secondary oocyte. In short, each secondary spermatocyte will produce 2 sperms so 50 secondary spermatocytes will produce 100 sperms (2*50). At the same time, each secondary oocyte will give only one Ova so 50 secondary oocytes = 50 ova.
